heading: Access to broadband telecommunications services in rural areas
label: 950bb Access to broadband telecommunications services in rural areas

Amendments2018—Subsec. (a). , substituted “provide grants, provide loans, and provide loan guarantees” for “provide loans and loan guarantees”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(1)Subsec. (b)(3)(A)(ii). , inserted “in the case of a grant or direct loan,” before “a city”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(2)Subsec. (b)(3)(C). , added subpar. (C).Pub. L. 115–334, § 6301(b)Subsec. (c). , substituted “Grants, loans, and” for “Loans and” in heading.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(3)(A)Subsec. (c)(1). , substituted “shall make grants, shall make loans, and shall guarantee loans” for “shall make or guarantee loans”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(3)(B)Subsec. (c)(2) to (4). , added pars. (2) to (4) and struck out former par. (2) which related to priority in making loans or loan guarantees.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(3)(C)Subsec. (d)(1)(A). , substituted “grant, loan, or” for “loan or” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(A)(i)(I)Subsec. (d)(1)(A)(i). , added cl. (i) and struck out former cl. (i) which read as follows: “demonstrate the ability to furnish, improve in order to meet the minimum acceptable level of broadband service established under subsection (e), or extend broadband service to all or part of an unserved rural area or an area below the minimum acceptable level of broadband service established under subsection (e);”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(A)(i)(II)Subsec. (d)(1)(A)(ii). , substituted “an application” for “a loan application”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(A)(i)(III)Subsec. (d)(1)(A)(iii). , struck out “loan” before “application” and substituted “infrastructure” for “service”, “5” for “3”, and “assistance under this section is” for “proceeds from the loan made or guaranteed under this section are”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(A)(i)(IV)Subsec. (d)(1)(B). , substituted “(j)” for “(k)”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(A)(ii)Subsec. (d)(2)(A). , substituted “assistance” for “the proceeds of a loan made or guaranteed” and “of the eligible entity” for “for the loan or loan guarantee” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(B)(i)Subsec. (d)(2)(A)(i). , substituted “50 percent (in the case of loans or loan guarantees provided in accordance with subsection (g)(1)(A))” for “15 percent” and “level of fixed broadband service, whether terrestrial or wireless,” for “level of broadband service”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(B)(ii)Subsec. (d)(3)(A). , substituted “grant, loan, or” for “loan or”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(C)Subsec. (d)(4). , substituted “assistance” for “a loan or loan guarantee”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(D)Subsec. (d)(5) to (10). , added par. (5) and struck out former pars. (5) to (10), which related to notice requirements, paperwork reduction, preapplication process, reporting by entities receiving assistance under this section, loan default and deobligation of awards, and service area assessment, respectively.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(4)(E)Subsec. (e)(1)(A). , substituted “25-Mbps” for “4-Mbps”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(5)(A)(i)Subsec. (e)(1)(B). , substituted “3-Mbps” for “1-Mbps”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(5)(A)(ii)Subsec. (e)(2). , struck out subpar. (A) designation and heading before “At least once”, inserted “and broadband buildout requirements under paragraph (4)” after “(1)”, and struck out subpar. (B). Prior to amendment, text of subpar. (B) read as follows: “In making an adjustment to the minimum acceptable level of broadband service under subparagraph (A), the Secretary may consider establishing different transmission rates for fixed broadband service and mobile broadband service.”Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(5)(B)Subsec. (e)(4). , added par. (4).Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(5)(C)Subsec. (f). , substituted “provide assistance” for “make a loan or loan guarantee”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(6)Subsec. (g)(2), (3). , redesignated par. (3) as (2) and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text of par. (2) read as follows: “In determining the term and conditions of a loan or loan guarantee, the Secretary may—Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(7)“(A) consider whether the recipient is or would be serving an area that is unserved or has service levels below the minimum acceptable level of broadband service established under subsection (e); and“(B) if the Secretary makes a determination in the affirmative under subparagraph (A), establish a limited initial deferral period or comparable terms necessary to achieve the financial feasibility and long-term sustainability of the project.”Subsec. (i). , added subsec. (i) and struck out former subsec. (i). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, the proceeds of any loan made or guaranteed by the Secretary under this chapter may be used by the recipient of the loan for the purpose of refinancing an outstanding obligation of the recipient on another telecommunications loan made under this chapter if the use of the proceeds for that purpose will support the construction, improvement, or acquisition of facilities and equipment for the provision of broadband service in rural areas.”Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(8)Subsec. (j). , (11), redesignated subsec. (k) as (j) and struck out former subsec. (j) which related to reports to Congress on participation in loan and loan guarantee program.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(8)Subsec. (k). , redesignated subsec. () as (k). Former subsec. (k) redesignated (j).Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(11)lSubsec. (k)(1). , substituted “$350,000,000” for “$25,000,000” and “2019 through 2023” for “2008 through 2018”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(9)Subsec. (). , redesignated subsec. () as (k).llPub. L. 115–334, § 6201(11), substituted “grant, or loan, or” for “loan or” and “2023” for “2018”.Pub. L. 115–334, § 6201(10)2014—Subsec. (c)(2). , added par. (2) and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In making or guaranteeing loans under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall give the highest priority to applicants that offer to provide broadband service to the greatest proportion of households that, prior to the provision of the broadband service, had no incumbent service provider.”Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(1)Subsec. (d)(1)(A)(i). , added cl. (i) and struck out former cl. (i). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “demonstrate the ability to furnish, improve, or extend a broadband service to a rural area;”.Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(A)Subsec. (d)(2)(A)(i). , added cl. (i) and struck out former cl. (i). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “not less than 25 percent of the households in the proposed service territory is offered broadband service by not more than 1 incumbent service provider; and”.Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(B)(i)Subsec. (d)(2)(B). , struck out “25” before “percent” in heading.Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(B)(ii)Subsec. (d)(2)(C). , struck out “3 or more” before “incumbent” in heading.Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(B)(iii)(I)Subsec. (d)(2)(C)(i). , added cl. (i) and struck out former cl.(i). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Except as provided in clause (ii), subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not apply to an incumbent service provider that is upgrading broadband service to the existing territory of the incumbent service provider.”Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(B)(iii)(II)Subsec. (d)(3)(B)(iii). , added cl. (iii).Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(C)Subsec. (d)(5). , added par. (5) and struck out former par. (5) which related to notice requirement.Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(D)Subsec. (d)(8) to (10). , added pars. (8) to (10).Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(2)(E)Subsec. (e). , added pars. (1) and (2), redesignated former par. (2) as (3), and struck out former par. (1) which read as follows: “The Secretary shall, from time to time as advances in technology warrant, review and recommend modifications of rate-of-data transmission criteria for purposes of the identification of broadband service technologies under subsection (b)(1).”Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(3)Subsec. (g)(2). , added par. (2) and struck out former par. (2). Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “In determining the term of a loan or loan guarantee, the Secretary shall consider whether the recipient is or would be serving an area that is not receiving broadband services.”Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(4)Subsec. (j)(1). , inserted “, including any loan terms or conditions for which the Secretary provided additional assistance to unserved areas” before semicolon at end.Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(5)(A)Subsec. (j)(7). –(D), added par. (7).Pub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(5)(B)Subsecs. (k)(1), (). , substituted “2018” for “2012”.lPub. L. 113–79, § 6104(a)(6)2008—, amended section generally, substituting provisions authorizing loans and loan guarantees for the costs of construction, improvement, and acquisition of facilities and equipment for broadband service in rural areas and terminating such authority on , for provisions authorizing similar loans and loan guarantees and terminating such authority on .Pub. L. 110–246, § 6110(a)Sept. 30, 2012Sept. 30, 20072004—Subsec. (b)(2). amended heading and text of subsec. (b)(2) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “The term ‘eligible rural community’ means any incorporated or unincorporated place that—Pub. L. 108–199“(A) has not more than 20,000 inhabitants, based on the most recent available population statistics of the Bureau of the Census; and“(B) is not located in an area designated as a standard metropolitan statistical area.”

Transition Rule, , , provided that:Pub. L. 115–334, title VI, § 6213132 Stat. 4746Dec. 20, 2018“For the period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act [] and ending on the date that is one year after such date of enactment, with respect to the implementation of the rural broadband access program under section 601 of the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 () and the Community Connect Grant Program under section 604 of such Act [], as added by section 6204 of this Act, the Secretary [of Agriculture] shall use the regulations in existence as of the day before the date of enactment of this Act that are applicable to the program involved, until the Secretary issues a final rule implementing the provisions of, and amendments made by, this title [see Tables for classification] that apply to that program.”Dec. 20, 20187 U.S.C. 950bb7 U.S.C. 950bb–3

L. 115–334, title VI, § 6213132 Stat. 4746Dec. 20, 2018“For the period beginning on the date of the enactment of this Act [] and ending on the date that is one year after such date of enactment, with respect to the implementation of the rural broadband access program under section 601 of the Rural Electrification Act of 1936 () and the Community Connect Grant Program under section 604 of such Act [], as added by section 6204 of this Act, the Secretary [of Agriculture] shall use the regulations in existence as of the day before the date of enactment of this Act that are applicable to the program involved, until the Secretary issues a final rule implementing the provisions of, and amendments made by, this title [see Tables for classification] that apply to that program.”Dec. 20, 20187 U.S.C. 950bb7 U.S.C. 950bb–3

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: a Purpose
children.text: The purpose of this section is to provide grants, provide loans, and provide loan guarantees to provide funds for the costs of the construction, improvement, and acquisition of facilities and equipment for broadband service in rural areas.

children.heading: Broadband service
children.label: 1 Broadband service
children.text: The term “broadband service” means any technology identified by the Secretary as having the capacity to transmit data to enable a subscriber to the service to originate and receive high-quality voice, data, graphics, and video.

children.heading: Incumbent service provider
children.label: 2 Incumbent service provider
children.text: The term “incumbent service provider”, with respect to an application submitted under this section, means an entity that, as of the date of submission of the application, is providing broadband service to not less than 5 percent of the households in the service territory proposed in the application.

children.label: i
children.text: an area described in clause (i) or (ii) of ; andsection 1991(a)(13)(A) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a grant or direct loan, a city, town, or incorporated area that has a population of greater than 20,000 inhabitants.

children.heading: Urban area growth
children.label: B Urban area growth
children.text: The Secretary may, by regulation only, consider an area described in to not be a rural area for purposes of this section.section 1991(a)(13)(F)(i)(I) of this title

children.heading: Exclusion of certain populations
children.label: C Exclusion of certain populations
children.text: Such term does not include any population described in subparagraph (H) or (I) of .section 1991(a)(13) of this title

children.heading: Grants, loans, and loan guarantees
children.label: c Grants, loans, and loan guarantees

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall make grants, shall make loans, and shall guarantee loans to eligible entities described in subsection (d) to provide funds for the construction, improvement, or acquisition of facilities and equipment for the provision of broadband service in rural areas.

children.label: I
children.text: a 10-Mbps downstream transmission capacity; and

children.label: II
children.text: a 1-Mbps upstream transmission capacity;

children.label: ii
children.text: give priority to applications for projects to provide the maximum level of broadband service to the greatest proportion of rural households in the proposed service area identified in the application;

children.label: iii
children.text: provide equal consideration to all eligible entities, including those that have not previously received grants, loans, or loan guarantees under paragraph (1); and

children.label: iv
children.text: with respect to 2 or more applications that are given the same priority under clause (i), give priority to an application that requests less grant funding than loan funding.

children.label: I
children.text: with a population of less than 10,000 permanent residents;

children.label: II
children.text: that are experiencing outmigration and have adopted a strategic community investment plan under that includes considerations for improving and expanding broadband service;section 2008v(d) of this title

children.label: III
children.text: with a high percentage of low income families or persons (as defined in );section 1471(b) of title 42

children.label: IV
children.text: that are isolated from other significant population centers; or

children.label: V
children.text: that provide rapid and expanded deployment of fixed and mobile broadband on cropland and ranchland within a service territory for use in various applications of precision agriculture; and

children.label: I
children.text: State, local, and tribal governments;

children.label: II
children.text: nonprofit institutions;

children.label: aa
children.text: public libraries;

children.label: bb
children.text: elementary schools and secondary schools (as defined in );section 7801 of title 20

children.label: cc
children.text: institutions of higher education; and

children.label: dd
children.text: health care facilities;

children.label: IV
children.text: private entities;

children.label: V
children.text: philanthropic organizations; and

children.label: VI
children.text: cooperatives.

children.label: i
children.text: construction, including labor and materials;

children.label: ii
children.text: project applications; and

children.label: iii
children.text: other development activities, as determined by the Secretary.

children.label: i
children.text: be carried out in a proposed service territory in which not less than 90 percent of the households are unserved; and

children.label: ii
children.text: not concurrently receive any other broadband grant administered by the Rural Utilities Service.

children.label: i
children.text: 75 percent of the total project cost with respect to an area with a density of fewer than 7 people per square mile;

children.label: ii
children.text: 50 percent of the total project cost with respect to an area with a density of 7 or more and fewer than 12 people per square mile; and

children.label: iii
children.text: 25 percent of the total project cost with respect to an area with a density of 12 or more and 20 or fewer people per square mile.

children.label: I
children.text: an area of rural households described in paragraph (2)(A)(i); or

children.label: II
children.text: a rural community described in any of subclauses (I) through (IV) of paragraph (2)(B)(i); and

children.label: ii
children.text: make modifications of the density thresholds described in subparagraph (C), in order to ensure that funds provided under this section are best utilized to provide broadband service in communities that are the most rural in character.

children.label: i
children.text: permits a single application for a grant and a loan under subchapter I, II, or this subchapter that is associated with such grant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: provides a single decision to award such grant and such loan.

children.label: i
children.text: utilizes publicly available data; and

children.label: ii
children.text: includes only those areas in which the applicant is able to meet the service requirements under this section, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Fees
children.label: 4 Fees
children.text: In the case of loan guarantees issued or modified under this section, the Secretary shall charge and collect from the lender fees in such amounts as to bring down the costs of subsidies for guaranteed loans, except that such fees shall not act as a bar to participation in the programs nor be inconsistent with current practices in the marketplace.

children.label: i
children.text: demonstrate the ability to furnish or improve service in order to meet the broadband buildout requirements established under subsection (e)(4) in all or part of an unserved or underserved rural area;

children.label: ii
children.text: submit to the Secretary an application at such time, in such manner, and containing such information as the Secretary may require; and

children.label: iii
children.text: agree to complete buildout of the broadband infrastructure described in the application by not later than 5 years after the initial date on which assistance under this section is made available.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: B Limitation
children.text: An eligible entity that provides telecommunications or broadband service to at least 20 percent of the households in the United States may not receive an amount of funds under this section for a fiscal year in excess of 15 percent of the funds authorized and appropriated under subsection (j) for the fiscal year.

children.label: i
children.text: not less than 50 percent (in the case of loans or loan guarantees provided in accordance with subsection (g)(1)(A)) of the households in the proposed service territory are unserved or have service levels below the minimum acceptable level of fixed broadband service, whether terrestrial or wireless, established under subsection (e); and

children.label: ii
children.text: broadband service is not provided in any part of the proposed service territory by 3 or more incumbent service providers.

children.heading: Exception to percent requirement
children.label: B Exception to percent requirement
children.text: Subparagraph (A)(i) shall not apply to the proposed service territory of a project if a loan or loan guarantee has been made under this section to the applicant to provide broadband service in the proposed service territory.

children.heading: Exception to incumbent service provider requirement
children.label: C Exception to incumbent service provider requirement

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), subparagraph (A)(ii) shall not apply to an incumbent service provider in the portion of a proposed service territory in which the provider is upgrading broadband service to meet the minimum acceptable level of broadband service established under subsection (e) for the existing territory of the incumbent service provider.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply if the applicant is eligible for funding under another subchapter of this chapter.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may require an entity to provide a cost share in an amount not to exceed 10 percent of the amount of the grant, loan, or loan guarantee requested in the application of the entity, unless the Secretary determines that a higher percentage is required for financial feasibility.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Secretary may require an entity that proposes to have a subscriber projection of more than 20 percent of the broadband service market in a rural area to submit to the Secretary a market survey.

children.heading: Less than 20 percent
children.label: ii Less than 20 percent
children.text: The Secretary may not require an entity that proposes to have a subscriber projection of less than 20 percent of the broadband service market in a rural area to submit to the Secretary a market survey.

children.label: I
children.text: certified by the affected community, city, county, or designee; or

children.label: aa
children.text: the broadband map of the affected State if the map contains address-level data; or

children.label: bb
children.text: the National Broadband Map if address-level data is unavailable.

children.heading: State and local governments and Indian tribes
children.label: 4 State and local governments and Indian tribes
children.text: Subject to paragraph (1), a State or local government (including any agency, subdivision, or instrumentality thereof (including consortia thereof)) and an Indian tribe shall be eligible for assistance under this section to provide broadband services to a rural area.

children.label: i
children.text: to prepare reports and surveys necessary to request grants, loans, and loan guarantees under this section for broadband deployment;

children.label: ii
children.text: to improve management, including financial management, relating to the proposed broadband deployment;

children.label: iii
children.text: to prepare applications for grants, loans, and loan guarantees under this section; or

children.label: iv
children.text: to assist with other areas of need identified by the Secretary.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: B Funding
children.text: Not less than 3 percent and not more than 5 percent of amounts appropriated to carry out this section for a fiscal year shall be used for technical assistance and training under this paragraph.

children.label: A
children.text: a 25-Mbps downstream transmission capacity; and

children.label: B
children.text: a 3-Mbps upstream transmission capacity.

children.heading: Adjustments
children.label: 2 Adjustments
children.text: At least once every 2 years, the Secretary shall review, and may adjust through notice published in the Federal Register, the minimum acceptable level of broadband service established under paragraph (1) and broadband buildout requirements under paragraph (4) to ensure that high quality, cost-effective broadband service is provided to rural areas over time.

children.heading: Prohibition
children.label: 3 Prohibition
children.text: The Secretary shall not establish requirements for bandwidth or speed that have the effect of precluding the use of evolving technologies appropriate for rural areas.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “broadband buildout requirement” means the level of internet service an applicant receiving assistance under this section must agree, at the time the application is finalized, to provide for the duration of any project-related agreement between the applicant and the Department.

children.heading: Broadband buildout requirements further defined
children.label: B Broadband buildout requirements further defined
i
children.text: 5 to 10 years;

children.label: ii
children.text: 11 to 15 years;

children.label: iii
children.text: 16 to 20 years; and

children.label: iv
children.text: more than 20 years.

children.label: i
children.text: utilize the same metrics used to define the minimum acceptable level of broadband service under paragraph (1);1So in original. Probably should be followed by “and”.1

children.label: I
children.text: the repayment of all loans and loan guarantees; and

children.label: II
children.text: the financed network is technically capable of providing broadband service for the lifetime of any project-related agreement.

children.heading: Substitute service standards for unique service territories
children.label: D Substitute service standards for unique service territories
children.text: If an applicant shows that it would be cost prohibitive to meet the broadband buildout requirements established under this paragraph for the entirety of a proposed service territory due to the unique characteristics of the proposed service territory, the Secretary and the applicant may agree to utilize substitute standards for any unserved portion of the project. Any substitute service standards should continue to consider the best technology available to meet the needs of the residents in the unserved area.

children.heading: Technological neutrality
children.label: f Technological neutrality
children.text: For purposes of determining whether to provide assistance for a project under this section, the Secretary shall use criteria that are technologically neutral.

children.heading: Terms and conditions for loans and loan guarantees
children.label: g Terms and conditions for loans and loan guarantees
I
children.text: the cost of borrowing to the Department of the Treasury for obligations of comparable maturity; or

children.label: II
children.text: 4 percent; and

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a guaranteed loan, the current applicable market rate for a loan of comparable maturity; and

children.label: B
children.text: have a term of such length, not exceeding 35 years, as the borrower may request, if the Secretary determines that the loan is adequately secured.

children.heading: Recurring revenue
children.label: 2 Recurring revenue
children.text: The Secretary shall consider the existing recurring revenues of the entity at the time of application in determining an adequate level of credit support.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the type and amount of, and method of security used to secure, any loan or loan guarantee under this section is commensurate to the risk involved with the loan or loan guarantee, particularly in any case in which the loan or loan guarantee is issued to a financially strong and stable entity, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Determination of amount and method of security
children.label: 2 Determination of amount and method of security
children.text: In determining the amount of, and method of security used to secure, a loan or loan guarantee under this section, the Secretary shall consider reducing the security in a rural area that does not have broadband service.

children.heading: Payment assistance for certain loan and grant recipients
children.label: i Payment assistance for certain loan and grant recipients

children.heading: Use of grant funds
children.label: 1 Use of grant funds
children.text: The Secretary may use the funds appropriated for a grant under this subchapter for the cost (as defined by ) of providing assistance under paragraph (2).section 661a of title 2

children.label: A
children.text: a subsidized loan, which shall bear a reduced interest rate at such a rate as the Secretary determines appropriate to meet the objectives of the program; or

children.label: I
children.text: in material compliance with the loan agreement; and

children.label: II
children.text: meeting the milestones and objectives of the project agreed to under paragraph (3); and

children.label: ii
children.text: require such nominal periodic payments as the Secretary determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Agreement on milestones and objectives
children.label: 3 Agreement on milestones and objectives
children.text: With respect to payment assistance provided under paragraph (2), before entering into the agreement under which the payment assistance will be provided, the applicant and the Secretary shall agree to milestones and objectives of the project.

children.heading: Amendment of milestones and objectives
children.label: 4 Amendment of milestones and objectives
children.text: The Secretary and the applicant may jointly agree to amend the milestones and objectives agreed to under paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: improve the compliance of the grantee with any commitments made through the grant agreement;

children.label: B
children.text: promote the completion of the broadband project;

children.label: C
children.text: protect taxpayer resources; and

children.label: D
children.text: support the integrity of the broadband programs administered by the Secretary.

children.heading: Limitations on payment assistance
children.label: 6 Limitations on payment assistance
children.text: The Secretary may not make a payment assistance loan under paragraph (2)(B) to an entity receiving a grant under this section that is also the recipient of a loan under subchapter I or II that is associated with such grant.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 1 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary to carry out this section $350,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2019 through 2023, to remain available until expended.

children.label: i
children.text: establish a national reserve for loans and loan guarantees to eligible entities in States under this section; and

children.label: ii
children.text: allocate amounts in the reserve to each State for each fiscal year for loans and loan guarantees to eligible entities in the State.

children.label: i
children.text: the number of communities with a population of 2,500 inhabitants or less in the State; bears to

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of communities with a population of 2,500 inhabitants or less in all States.

children.heading: Unobligated amounts
children.label: C Unobligated amounts
children.text: Any amounts in the reserve established for a State for a fiscal year under subparagraph (B) that are not obligated by April 1 of the fiscal year shall be available to the Secretary to make loans and loan guarantees under this section to eligible entities in any State, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: Termination of authority
children.label: k Termination of authority
children.text: No grant, or loan, or loan guarantee may be made under this section after .September 30, 2023
